# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Linkerd default authorization policy for a namespace. Configure policy to handle services without explicit authorization rules. Set up default behavior for service-to-service communication. This is for our service mesh policy framework.
# Category: linkerd_policy_default_allow

apiVersion: linkerd.io/v1alpha2
kind: ServiceProfile
metadata:
  name: namespace_defaultAuthzPolicy
spec:
  selectorRef:
    kind: Namespace # Replace with the appropriate service for your use case, ie., KubernetesNamespace or any other specific resource type you are using. Default is 'KubernetesNamespace' if not specified otherwise by linkerd-injector process in injecting configuration into namespace/configmap
    name: default   # Replace with the actual Namespace that your policy should be set for, replace all lower case letters and numbers to match K8s namespaces like 'default', 'myNamespace1' etc. The value will depend on context of running kubectl commands or where these resources are being deployed
  attachments: # Attach the linkerd configuration (e.g., policies, virtual services) here for this service profile to apply at creation time in namespace specified by metadata name field above and then applied as they come online   -- optional if not included you can also include rules-based annotations below where there is a section of yaml that would be injected
    attachments: # List the configurations or resources here for linkerd to apply at creation time. For example, policies are defined by kind/name pairs in this list and applied as they become available  -- optional if not included you can also include rules-based annotations below where there is a section of yaml that would be injected
    - |+ # Required field for each item being attached (mandatory)   ---- This line has only one hyphen in front. It's mandatory so it should start with plus sign '+'. The rest must not have any '-' present and is required to complete the yaml document below which this block of code belongs
      kind: VirtualService  # Required (mandatory)   ---- This line has only one hyphen in front. It's mandatory so it should start with plus sign '+'. The rest must not have any '-' present and is required to complete the yaml document below which this block of code belongs
      metadata:
        name: default-virtualservice  # Required (mandatory)   ---- This line has only one hyphen in front. It's mandatory so it should start with plus sign '+'. The rest must not have any '-' present and is required to complete the yaml document below which this block of code belongs
      spec:     --- Optional if you want all rules or service definitions at once (excludes services, routes etc.)   ---- Useful only when using a policy framework that supports defining/configuring these items as part-of declarations. In other cases use full config and annotations for the entire content of VirtualService section below this block
      --- This is used to define rules or service definitions by setting them in separate files at configuration time   ---- Useful when you want a more complex resource that requires defined multiple parts (e.g., routes, services etc.)  In other cases use full annotations for the entire content of VirtualService section below this block
